question: Should swarm formalize two-speed coverage as policy (explicit revival protocol for dormant domains every ~30 sessions) or fight it with hard floors?
hypothesis: Era Gini has continued improving below 0.45 by S441; revival protocol unnecessary
method: Parse SWARM-LANES.md for all DOMEX lanes opened in S428-S441 (14-session window); compute Gini coefficient over domain visit distribution
verdict: TARGET ACHIEVED. Era Gini 0.752→0.646→0.475→0.425. UCB1 natural equilibrium works without explicit revival protocol. Target <0.45 reached S441 (20s later than S415-S420 prediction). Hard 20% visit floor is sufficient guard. Economy domain (0 visits S428-S440) correctly received high UCB1 explore score and was dispatched this session - demonstrating self-correction without forced revival.
f_eco6_resolution: RESOLVED: revival protocol not needed. UCB1 explore term naturally corrects over-concentration on previously dormant domains. Recommendation: maintain current UCB1+floor approach. No revival protocol needed.
note_on_concentration: Top 4 domains (META=8, NK=6, CAT=6, EXP=5) = 62.5% of visits. Two-speed pattern persists but Gini below threshold. This is healthy productive concentration, not pathological over-exploitation.
diff_from_expect: Confirmed: era Gini 0.425 < 0.45 target. Two-speed amplitude decreasing as predicted. Economy self-dispatched this session without forced revival - UCB1 self-corrects.
